74519.subject: Companies are scrambling to curtail soaring AI costs
74519.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAArQPqlOmprpzGJzcBTeG4wPwzTCY5pMDUwYzNE_wkWeUdYHSTyt2lTI2vekiJ1hS-lop5Z23OjSpGXxphQVgR-Yrc2ehXxRhWGylRO2Q48NaBQ 

June 14th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 A Sunday edition of our daily newsletter


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAArQPqlOn94EwgGk0gtgEKcBrD70gCPx654WoQTOtnpOTAKPcZV8EgAweHSD-3kql7-KZB6Nn2cj3V-crkBGEdhW2_m2SKcWKtID5_JDp-EGwCg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAArQPqlOoXoJjKGry116Q-4JM9Q5zQgL2gTFJMn3hwNFwKBRpSe5gKRqHX40-lOl_JQYrLUSonfYCPII6E4SBd1DMYQe6EgaUNQbllou682PHdg 



 
 

Roger McShane

Deputy US editor



Until recently bosses encouraged employees to binge on artificial intelligence, with spending seen as a sign of innovation. Employees could even take pride in burning through tokens—the chunks of text that models process and a common unit of AI pricing. Now, though, companies are scrambling to rein in 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAArQPqlOp-a4QumfkrTxGf3gU20rs3v4AvSMq6YuCGgRhHgDyngH8EoHveOSJT7UbovepkXf8M1tYgiO4gCoivVMK7eYGMfzm5B4KYaHA 
soaring AI bills . Tokenmaxxing is no longer a badge of honour.


And we’ve previously covered the Donroe doctrine—
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAArQPqlOqlsur-UtlHK_xSET9-EAmC1a_K1hJUpr51qIIPTVDcyKMpgnAeyJFXwy94y3DLH9QV

74518.subject: Revolut’s $115B secondary share sale mints Europe’s first centicorn 🇪🇺🦄; Vinyl raised $20M to replace the fax m…
74518.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/weeklyfintechpulse403

👋 Hey, Linas here! Welcome back to a 🔓 weekly free edition 🔓 of my daily newsletter. Each day, I focus on 3 stories that are making a difference in the financial technology space. Coupled with things worth watching & most important money movements, it’s the only newsletter you need for all things when Finance meets Tech.
Join the Future of Finance & AI:
If you’re not a subscriber, here’s what you missed this week:
Everything Anthropic Shipped in 2026: Every Claude Model, Agent, & Tool - And How to Actually Use Them [ https://substack.com/redirect/55734bdc-f9e0-4c02-bda1-ecf0e73e7421?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] 🤖 [ https://substack.com/redirect/55734bdc-f9e0-4c02-bda1-ecf0e73e7421?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] [29 Claude launches in 5 months. Most users missed half of them. Here’s every model, agent, and tool - with the playbook for each one]
Loop Engineering: How to Design AI Loops That Build, Ship, and Improve While You Sleep [ https://substack.com/redirect/726d9abe-8b9f-46ff-9591-cf291465cd5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] 🔁 [ https://substack.com/redirect/726d9abe-8b9f-46ff-9591-cf291465cd5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] [From a three-line bash script to multi-day Claude Fable 5 autonomy — everything founders, builders, and investors need to stop prompting agents and start designing the systems that prompt them]
The Complete Guide to Claude Dynamic Workflows [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bc551d5-0a50-4de8-847c-f1155b01e65b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] 🤖 [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bc551d5-0a50-4de8-847c-f1155b01e65b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] [From first principles to production: every workflow pattern, cost trap, and real-world use cases founders, builders, and investors need to master Anthropic’s most powerful Claude Code feature yet]
You’re Prompting Claude Fable 5 & Mythos 5 Wrong. You Have 10 Days to Fix That [ https://substack.com/redirect/347b798d-467a-43ac-aa60-c8ac427e1f69?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] 🤖 [ https://substack.com/redirect/347b798d-467a-43ac-aa60-c8ac427e1f69?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] [The complete Fable 5 playbook for founders, builders, & investors — 12 prompting patterns, the orchestrator architecture, effort economics, and everything you need built before free plan ends 22 June]
The Claude Finance Playbook: How CFOs Use AI to Build Models, Forecast Cash, and Close Books Faster [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bf72618-1d49-43a0-a5a8-d46ed1045cef?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] 📊 [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bf72618-1d49-43a0-a5a8-d46ed1045cef?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUD

74516.subject: 📰 AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest — June 14
74516.body:
AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: CryptoBeholder.com


Автор: CryptoBeholder.com

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-14-cryptobeholder-t35pf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B0Nhjp8urQvuTXwiCzAn1vw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3vrdyj7E8K3ck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqe0o02q~3x-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqe0o02q-3x
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: A July 1 MiCA cutoff looms for millions of EU crypto users while a DPRK-linked $36M hack and a UK naval seizure exposing USDT sanctions evasion show that crypto's geopolitical risk is no longer theoretical. 🚨 Millions of EU Crypto Users Face Exchange Cutoff as MiCA Deadline Hits in Days On July 1, 2026, the temporary operating permission that allowed crypto companies to serve EU customers while awaiting a full MiCA license expires — and exchanges, brokers, and wallet services that have not secured authorization must immediately cut off access to European users with no further grace period. CryptoSlate reports that the deadline creates a live operational crisis for ordinary investors who may lose access to their funds, positions, or platforms overnight, with little notice from providers scrambling to reach compliance. Read full story 💀 Humanity Protocol's $36M Hack Linked to Suspected North Korean Hackers Cybersecurity firm Quantstamp has attributed the $36 million breach of Humanity Protocol to suspected North Korean threat actors, identifying a fraudulent phishing email spoofing crypto exchange Bithumb as the attack's primary entry vector. The incident adds another eight-figure sum to the growing ledger of assets stolen by DPRK-affiliated hacking groups, which have systematically targeted blockchain projects to fund state programs operating under international sanctions. More details ⚖️ CFTC No-Action Letter Opens First Legal Path for Digital Commodity Perpetuals in the US The CFTC issued a staff no-action letter that clears a legal runway for perpetual futures contracts on digital commodities to operate within the United States — a product category that has until now existed almost exclusively at offshore venues to sidestep regulatory uncertainty. The guidance provides immediate operational clarity for US-domiciled exchanges and their institutional clients, who have long been unable to offer the perpetual swap structures that dominate global crypto derivatives volumes. Read more 📊 SEC Proposes Reg NMS Rule Changes That Could Reshape Tokenized Stock Trading The SEC has proposed rescinding Rules 611 and 610e of Regulation NMS — the order-protection and access rules that have governed US equity market structure for two decades — in a move with substantial downstream implications for how tok

74511.subject: Well Informed: Have scientists unlocked a master switch for cancer?
74511.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAAq6D5KgpRiFScsiq8AShYINZwKonu-Gty4OEsXuH2xKxt4l-37hvgYKBhLdIZoxZMtSgSHuoizOL1do0hxtP4uqtkrIzXswIOF3hgjktDikO 

June 14th 2026

 

 
 
 
Well Informed

 Your evidence-based guide to health and wellness


 
 
 






 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAAq6D5KgqOZonUIjlVdTXYY5hCSU9kQ6rF__dqkBKpX7ryjwRQl_YbKYlY6enDFOl4cSEbYJvMhuOdAl7R4GDnO7U6nxR8YaNmTXkvT3RH2Kp 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAAq6D5Kgr7jgirEA4VgFaS1srrakh58qztpMXAG5kylyTpYqAnuFUBUmbdX-Hy-eLuBJrvUZdU4WZBcAB5Z6GwHIGrY-Iym3oYYSvE_X_2NdU 


Have scientists unlocked a master switch for cancer?


Tim Cross

Senior science writer


Almost a year ago we ran a 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAAq6D5KgsnWBNdaTTc5K_G5iuo0L0LVha1Ir8Pt7-Ks57747PvP0cunuUKMp6s_gX022cz30Wbb6cqa6Lxrmc0aC7tuqaYaN7d_ouwoQ 
cover story  highlighting the slow, grinding—but very real—progress that the world has made in the “War on Cancer” since Richard Nixon, then America’s president, first declared it in 1971. As we wrote at the time, good news often goes unreported, especially when it happens gradually, as it has with cancer.

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MDd9AAcAAAAAAq6D5Kgt_KgfaqQNT5qjn9nFvpqEgQs6sVliuODet-xM8BEIh1l7i0tD_M6hWMCf7fw7P6Jjgwekp4OPGS5o2HAdbWZ1x4
